page: Amazon Web Services r6i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64 with hyperthreading enabled. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 61949.73 and a PassMark CPU score of 57729.17, both placing in the top 25% tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, which contributes to strong performance in PassMark encryption and software compilation. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show average tier performance, with read-only large block bandwidth at 167.36 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 67.73 GB/sec.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1 makes this instance class suitable for memory-intensive workloads. PassMark database operations scored in the top 25% at 26616.49, indicating strong performance for relational databases and in-memory data stores, though Redis SET operations performed in the average tier at 55.03 million operations per second. The lack of local storage requires network-attached storage, which introduces a dependency on network performance for I/O-heavy tasks. This instance provides high resource density for enterprise applications, database hosting, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r6i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro System, this x86_64 server delivers 8.0 GB of memory per physical core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 57729.17 and strong database operations performance. Single-core metrics and memory bandwidth fall within the average tier, with large-block read bandwidth at 167.36 GB/sec. The instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring high memory capacity relative to compute resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C CPU, designed for database operations and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and strong multi-core processing for database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512 GB DDR4 RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8375C at 3.5 GHz
Built on the AWS Nitro System with 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark performance
Support for AVX-512 and hardware-accelerated cryptography
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for memory-intensive databases and enterprise applications
